merged_findings.finding: Phase 6 lists `tests/benchmark/test_cli_probe.py` as MODIFY ('replaces Phase 4's initial test') but no prior phase creates this file. Phase 4 creates `tests/benchmark/test_recognition_probe.py`, not `test_cli_probe.py`. Phase 6 will fail attempting to modify a nonexistent file.

claude.finding: Phase 6 lists `tests/benchmark/test_cli_probe.py` as MODIFY ('replaces Phase 4's initial test') but no prior phase creates this file. Phase 4 creates `tests/benchmark/test_recognition_probe.py`, not `test_cli_probe.py`. Phase 6 will fail attempting to modify a nonexistent file.

merged_findings.finding: Venv lock timeout (10-min ceiling) is scored as `install_failed` — a model failure outcome — but it is actually harness resource contention between concurrent orchestrators. Under high concurrency (two `br benchmark run` sessions on Muddy), one model could be systematically penalized if its runs happen to queue behind the other's long-running pytest, introducing a confound the regression does not control for.

claude.finding: Venv lock timeout (10-min ceiling) is scored as `install_failed` — a model failure outcome — but it is actually harness resource contention between concurrent orchestrators. Under high concurrency (two `br benchmark run` sessions on Muddy), one model could be systematically penalized if its runs happen to queue behind the other's long-running pytest, introducing a confound the regression does not control for.

merged_findings.finding: `git clone --local --shared --no-hardlinks`: `--no-hardlinks` is redundant when combined with `--shared`. `--shared` sets up `.git/objects/info/alternates` and skips all object copying, so `--no-hardlinks` has no effect. Not harmful, but signals possible misunderstanding of the clone's object-sharing semantics — worth verifying the author's assumption that the clone is 'fully mutable without affecting the parent' holds when alternates are in use (it does, but only for new objects; a `git gc` or `git repack` in the parent while a clone exists can break the clone).

claude.finding: `git clone --local --shared --no-hardlinks`: `--no-hardlinks` is redundant when combined with `--shared`. `--shared` sets up `.git/objects/info/alternates` and skips all object copying, so `--no-hardlinks` has no effect. Not harmful, but signals possible misunderstanding of the clone's object-sharing semantics — worth verifying the author's assumption that the clone is 'fully mutable without affecting the parent' holds when alternates are in use (it does, but only for new objects; a `git gc` or `git repack` in the parent while a clone exists can break the clone).

merged_findings.finding: BinomialBayesMixedGLM with variational Bayes (`fit_vb()`) used for phase-level random intercepts: with only 30 groups (phases) in the full study and 7 in the pilot, VB approximations to random-intercept posteriors are known to underestimate between-group variance. The acknowledged caveat ('may be anticonservative') could be severe enough at N=7 pilot to render pilot-stage mixed-model results misleading. The cluster-robust fixed-effects logit fallback is the safer default at pilot scale.

claude.finding: BinomialBayesMixedGLM with variational Bayes (`fit_vb()`) used for phase-level random intercepts: with only 30 groups (phases) in the full study and 7 in the pilot, VB approximations to random-intercept posteriors are known to underestimate between-group variance. The acknowledged caveat ('may be anticonservative') could be severe enough at N=7 pilot to render pilot-stage mixed-model results misleading. The cluster-robust fixed-effects logit fallback is the safer default at pilot scale.

merged_findings.finding: Below `/api/classify` calibration uses 20 pairs with a 16/20 (80%) agreement threshold. The exact 95% Clopper-Pearson CI for 16/20 is [56.3%, 94.3%] — wide enough that a validator with true agreement as low as 57% could pass the gate by chance. The plan discloses this as a limitation but does not size the calibration set to a target CI width.

claude.finding: Below `/api/classify` calibration uses 20 pairs with a 16/20 (80%) agreement threshold. The exact 95% Clopper-Pearson CI for 16/20 is [56.3%, 94.3%] — wide enough that a validator with true agreement as low as 57% could pass the gate by chance. The plan discloses this as a limitation but does not size the calibration set to a target CI width.

merged_findings.finding: Phase 1 defines `BenchmarkConfig.registry` with only `lease_seconds`, but Phase 6's judge-lock flow reads `config.registry.judge_lease_seconds`. That config field is never declared in the frozen Phase 1 schema, so the judge claim path cannot load its lease duration.

codex.finding: Phase 1 defines `BenchmarkConfig.registry` with only `lease_seconds`, but Phase 6's judge-lock flow reads `config.registry.judge_lease_seconds`. That config field is never declared in the frozen Phase 1 schema, so the judge claim path cannot load its lease duration.

merged_findings.finding: Phase 1's `test_installer_order.py` expects a synthetic repo with `pytest<9.0` and `pytest>=9.0` to install a pytest version satisfying the harness pin, but the same plan later states that this exact conflict should raise pip `ResolutionImpossible` and be scored as `install_failed`. Those requirements are mutually exclusive; the test and the runtime contract cannot both be correct.

codex.finding: Phase 1's `test_installer_order.py` expects a synthetic repo with `pytest<9.0` and `pytest>=9.0` to install a pytest version satisfying the harness pin, but the same plan later states that this exact conflict should raise pip `ResolutionImpossible` and be scored as `install_failed`. Those requirements are mutually exclusive; the test and the runtime contract cannot both be correct.

merged_findings.finding: The OpenRouter preflight contract is contradictory. In `session_state.py`'s Phase 1 description, startup is described as doing a live `GET /api/v1/models` check, while later `deps.py` deliverables explicitly say `/api/v1/models` is never called and only `POST /api/v1/chat/completions` probes are allowed. That leaves two incompatible implementations for the same preflight.

codex.finding: The OpenRouter preflight contract is contradictory. In `session_state.py`'s Phase 1 description, startup is described as doing a live `GET /api/v1/models` check, while later `deps.py` deliverables explicitly say `/api/v1/models` is never called and only `POST /api/v1/chat/completions` probes are allowed. That leaves two incompatible implementations for the same preflight.

merged_findings.finding: The GPG-signing preflight does not actually verify the condition it claims to guard. `ssh <node> 'git config --global --get commit.gpgsign || echo false'` only tells you whether signing is enabled, not whether a usable signing key exists. A node with `commit.gpgsign=true` and a broken/missing key will still pass this probe and then fail remote commits at runtime.

codex.finding: The GPG-signing preflight does not actually verify the condition it claims to guard. `ssh <node> 'git config --global --get commit.gpgsign || echo false'` only tells you whether signing is enabled, not whether a usable signing key exists. A node with `commit.gpgsign=true` and a broken/missing key will still pass this probe and then fail remote commits at runtime.
